_doc: World-region groupings + named presets for the league name picker.
A region either has flat first_keys/surname_keys (legacy shape) OR a list
of `subregions`. Subregions are the picker's atomic unit: when a draw lands
on a region with subregions, ONE subregion is chosen by weight and the
first name + surname are both drawn from THAT subregion's keys. This keeps
names culturally coherent — a Pakistani draw produces 'Babar Iqbal', not
'Babar Iyer' (which would happen if first/surname were sampled from the
concatenated South Asia pool).
`country` (or, for legacy regions, `country_weights`) gives the ISO 3166-1
alpha-2 country code stored on the player row. The web UI converts it to
a flag emoji at render time.
Presets are O27-thesis-shaped: the league launches in East Asia, then a
cricket-conversion pipeline floods the rosters with South Asian, Caribbean,
Australasian, African, Afghan and (most importantly) Malaysian players —
Malaysia being the structural growth market for the sport, the way Germany
is for football.
Subregioned by country so each youth-league nation pins to a
country-flavored pool via make_country_pinned_picker. Subregion
weights mirror the prior country_weights so the broad pro-league
use of latin_america: 1.0 keeps its country mix.
PR pulls from the curated puerto_rican pool (see male_first /
surnames JSON); the rest currently share latino_hispanic +
latin_american pending per-country curation.
latin_america.label: Latin America

_doc: ARGENTINA + BRAZIL are the secondary 'non-baseball powerhouse' axis.
Football-built athletic infrastructure redirects to O27, scouts arrive
before MLB saturates. Top-10 by player count, not per-capita —
their dominance comes from population scale, not the structural
over-production seen in Guyana/Trinidad.
Brazilian sub-pools deliberately surface hyphenated naming patterns
(Yamamoto-Costa, Bauer-Silva) that are genuinely distinct from
generic 'Latin American' MLB naming.
south_america.label: South America (non-baseball powerhouses)

east_asia.label: East Asia
_note: Decomposed into culturally cohesive subregions so a Japanese player
draws Japanese first + Japanese surname, not the cross-pollinated
'Yamamoto Kim' shape the old flat region produced. JP is the launch
market of O27 and stays the largest single subregion.

_doc: Indo-Fijian is the THIRD cricket-conversion country (after Guyana
and Trinidad) — Indo-Fijians have a separate cricket pipeline,
underutilised by the West Indies / Asia structure, that O27
reroutes. Held to ~0.18 of the region (smaller than native Fijian
by population but high per-capita talent yield, mirroring the
GY/TT pattern). Native Fijian, Samoan, Tongan, and Papuan
subregions decompose what was previously a flat pacific_islander
draw. Guam (Chamorro), American Samoa (reusing the Samoan pool)
and Hawaii (Native Hawaiian + Portuguese/Asian-American island
blend) extend the region into the real US-affiliated baseball
pipeline. Hawaii carries country code US — it is a US state, not
a separate nation.
pacific_islands.label: Pacific Islands

_doc: Dutch Caribbean — Curaçao/Aruba (Papiamento blend, real baseball
pedigree: Bogaerts, Profar, Jansen) and Suriname (Dutch +
Javanese + Hindustani + Maroon — arguably the most ethnically
diverse naming pool in the hemisphere). Distinct from the
Anglophone caribbean_cricket region: different language, different
pipeline, different naming conventions.
caribbean_dutch.label: Caribbean (Dutch)

_doc: GUYANA + TRINIDAD are the dual axis. Combined they hold 0.72 of
the region's mass — disproportionate to their populations relative
to JM/BB/AG, but matching the O27 thesis: the Indo-Caribbean cricket
pipeline is the highest-density talent producer in the league per
capita. Trinidad is decomposed into Indo / Afro / Chinese / Lebanese
subregions to reflect the country's actual demographic spread, which
is more complex than Guyana's bi-ethnic split.
caribbean_cricket.label: Caribbean (Cricket Anglophone)

_doc: CZECH REPUBLIC is the European baseball powerhouse — real-world
Czech baseball has been the breakout story (WBC 2023 qualification,
growing MLB-org presence). In O27 fiction the trend accelerates:
European football academies redirect to baseball faster in CZ than
anywhere else on the continent, and Czech players become the
single largest non-Anglophone European feeder. CZ holds 0.42 of
the region — more than PL+RU combined.
Other subregions decompose Eastern Europe properly: Ukraine,
Hungary, Slovakia, Romania, Bulgaria. Russia retained but
reduced — a real but secondary pipeline.
europe_eastern.label: Eastern Europe

_doc: Full national breakout: Sweden / Finland / Norway / Denmark each
draw from their own distinct given+surname pools (the -sson, -nen
and -sen patronymic belts are kept separate) rather than a single
blended Scandinavian pool. Weights match the old country split.
nordic.label: Nordic

_note: Decomposed into culturally cohesive subregions. The Philippines
is no longer a subregion here — it was promoted to its own
top-level `philippines` region (six cultural subregions). This
region now covers mainland + maritime SE Asia: Vietnam, Thailand,
Indonesia, Singapore (Chinese + Malay), Myanmar (mononymic
Burmese), Laos, Cambodia and Brunei (reusing the malay pool).
southeast_asia.label: Southeast Asia

_doc: Promoted from a single Spanish-colonial subregion of southeast_asia
to a full region. Filipino naming is Spanish-Catholic surnames over
Tagalog/regional given names — but the country is not monolithic:
Visayan and Ilocano lines carry distinct surname pools, the
Chinese-Filipino (Chinoy) community uses Hokkien-derived surnames
with mostly Western given names, the old landed Spanish-mestizo
families surface peninsular Spanish surnames, and Muslim Mindanao
(Maranao/Tausug/Maguindanao) is a wholly separate naming world.
Weights are a rough demographic split, not a baseball-talent claim.
philippines.label: Philippines

_doc: UAE & Oman associate-cricket sides are overwhelmingly South-Asian
expat communities (Indian + Pakistani) with a thin native-Emirati/
Omani Arab tail. Reuses the existing south-Asian and Arabic pools
rather than minting new buckets — the demographic IS the diaspora.
gulf_cricket.label: Gulf (expat cricket)

_doc: Real WBC baseball nation (qualified 2017 & 2023). Native Israeli
Hebrew given names + the Sephardi/Mizrahi/Ashkenazi surname blend.
israel.label: Israel

_doc: Established European baseball nation (Hoofdklasse, multiple WBC
appearances). Promoted out of the flat europe_western pool so a
Netherlands national side draws Dutch names tagged NL rather than
the mixed German/French/Spanish blend.
netherlands.label: Netherlands

_doc: Established European baseball nation (Serie A1, WBC regular).
Promoted out of europe_western so an Italy national side draws
Italian names tagged IT.
italy.label: Italy

_doc: Home of pesäpallo (Finnish baseball). Dedicated Finnish given/
family-name pool (the -nen surname belt: Virtanen, Korhonen,
Mäkinen), distinct from the Scandinavian neighbours.
finland.label: Finland

_doc: Swedish national pool — the -sson patronymic belt (Andersson,
Johansson, Karlsson).
sweden.label: Sweden

_doc: Norwegian national pool — the -sen patronymic belt (Hansen,
Johansen, Olsen) with the ø/å given names.
norway.label: Norway

_doc: Danish national pool — the -sen belt (Jensen, Nielsen, Hansen).
denmark.label: Denmark

_doc: The 'Puerto Rico of O27' — shares Hispaniola with the baseball-mad
Dominican Republic, so the same island pipeline feeds a distinct
Francophone naming world: French/Haitian-Creole given names over
French + saint-prefixed surnames (Saint-Fleur, Jean-Baptiste, Désir).
haiti.label: Haiti

_doc: Dutch-Caribbean baseball powerhouse (Bogaerts, Profar, Jansen).
Promoted out of caribbean_dutch to its own Papiamento pool.
curacao.label: Curacao

_doc: Dutch-Caribbean baseball nation alongside Curacao. Papiamento
given names with the Aruban surname set (Croes, Tromp, Maduro).
aruba.label: Aruba

_doc: The most ethnically diverse naming pool in the hemisphere:
Hindustani, Javanese, Maroon and Dutch-Creole lines, each with
its own given/surname set. Promoted out of caribbean_dutch.
suriname.label: Suriname

_doc: Anglophone South American cricket nation, bi-ethnic Indo-Guyanese
and Afro-Guyanese split. Promoted out of caribbean_cricket to its
own region.
guyana.label: Guyana

_doc: Pacific baseball nation (CHamoru). Spanish-colonial surnames over
CHamoru/Western given names.
guam.label: Guam

_doc: Southeast Asian growth market. Indonesian given/family names.
indonesia.label: Indonesia

_doc: Southeast Asian baseball/softball nation. Thai given/family names.
thailand.label: Thailand

_doc: Emerging European baseball nations outside the established DE/NL/
IT/CZ core: Greece (long-standing federation, 2004-Olympics host),
Croatia and Slovenia.
europe_southeast.label: Southeast Europe (emerging baseball)

_note: Decomposed by ethnic group within country. Nigeria splits Yoruba
/ Igbo / Hausa (visibly different surnames); Ethiopia splits
Amharic / Oromo; Ghana represented by Akan. The previous flat
pools (african, ethiopian, east_african, lusophone_african,
malagasy) remain available as fallbacks for the country-level
subregions that don't have their own ethnic decomposition yet
(TZ, MG, MZ, AO).
africa.label: Sub-Saharan Africa

_doc: Promoted out of south_america to its own region. Brazil's distinctive
naming is the hyphenated immigrant blend — Japanese-Brazilian,
Italian-Brazilian, German-Brazilian and Lebanese-Brazilian lines
(Yamamoto-Costa, Bauer-Silva) layered over the Portuguese base — which
is genuinely distinct from generic MLB-shaped Latin American naming.
Subregion weights are the south_america BR subregions renormalised to 1.0.
brazil.label: Brazil

_doc: Real baseball nation (LMB summer league, LMP winter league, WBC
regular). Promoted out of the latin_america MX subregion so a Mexico
national side draws names tagged MX rather than the blended Latin
American pool.
mexico.label: Mexico

_doc: Premier baseball nation. Promoted out of the latin_america CU
subregion (which only borrowed the generic latino_hispanic pool)
to a dedicated Cuban pool. The distinctive feature is the wave of
Y-initial given names (Yoenis, Yasiel, Yandy, Yulieski) that mark
post-1990s Cuban naming, over a Spanish + Afro-Cuban surname stock.
cuba.label: Cuba

_doc: The single largest non-US MLB feeder. Promoted out of the
latin_america DO subregion to a dedicated Dominican pool (García,
Tatís, Guerrero, Encarnación over the Vladimir/Wander/Starlin
given-name set).
dominican.label: Dominican Republic

_doc: Top-tier baseball nation (LVBP, second-largest MLB feeder after the
DR). Promoted out of the latin_america VE subregion to a dedicated
Venezuelan pool (Altuve, Cabrera, Aparicio, Concepción over the
Asdrúbal/Gleyber/Salvador given-name set).
venezuela.label: Venezuela

_doc: Promoted out of the central_west_asia blend. Turkish given + family
names tagged TR.
turkey.label: Turkey

_doc: Long-standing baseball federation (hosted the 2004 Olympic
tournament). Promoted out of europe_southeast to its own Greek pool.
greece.label: Greece

_doc: Promoted out of the europe_eastern blend. Russian given + family
names tagged RU.
russia.label: Russia

_doc: Promoted out of the europe_eastern blend. Ukrainian given + family
names tagged UA.
ukraine.label: Ukraine

o27_year_1.label: O27 Year 1 (Launch — East Asia + legacy baseball pipelines)
_note: Launch season: cricket-conversion academies announced but the player
pipeline hasn't matured. Roster mix is heavily MLB-shaped + a real
East-Asia presence (the launch market) and the first wave of cricket
early-adopters from South Asia, Australia, Caribbean, SA.
Guyana and Trinidad already over-represented within the (small)
caribbean_cricket allocation — see that region's subregion weights.
South America starts visible: Argentine and Brazilian football-
infrastructure converts faster than expected. Pelé's grandson
narrative.

o27_year_5.label: O27 Year 5 (Pipeline visible — academies producing pros)
_note: Mid-pipeline: cricket academies in Pakistan, India, Caribbean, SA
are graduating their first wave of legitimate O27 pros. Malaysia
growth visible. North American share retreats.
Guyana + Trinidad combined now ~70% of caribbean_cricket mass.
Argentine + Brazilian academies (football pipeline conversion)
produce first All-Stars; south_america cracks 10% — top-10 by
count, while GY+TT remain top-tier per capita.

o27_year_10.label: O27 Year 10 (Mature — full cricket-pipeline thesis)
_note: Mature league: the cricket-conversion thesis has played out. South
Asia is the largest single feeder, Malaysia is the structural
growth market, and the league looks globally rooted instead of an
MLB sim with the rules filed off.
Guyana + Trinidad combined are ~10% of total league players —
from countries with a combined population of ~2M. That's the
structural over-production: Cuba-of-O27 per capita, top-5 elite
producers in absolute terms. South America (Argentina + Brazil
anchor) is the top-10-by-scale story: ~13% of league, Brazil with
visibly hyphenated Italian-Brazilian / Japanese-Brazilian /
German-Brazilian rosters distinct from MLB-shaped Latin America.
